stories.headline: Colorado man dies at same Arapahoe County intersection where his wife was killed in 2024
stories.summary:
Gerald 'Gerry' Goldberg, who co-founded a community advocacy group after his wife Andreia was fatally struck while crossing East Belleview Avenue and South Franklin Street in May 2024, died Monday in a crash at that same intersection, the Arapahoe County coroner's office confirmed. The intersection sits on the border of Greenwood Village and Cherry Hills Village along Colorado 88, a state highway.

After his wife's death, Gerry Goldberg co-founded Andie's Light with former Greenwood Village councilmember Jerry Presley to petition local officials for a traffic signal at the dangerous crossing. He had addressed Greenwood Village City Council in December about the circumstances of his wife's death, describing how a driver failed to see her while she was crossing.

Following Goldberg's death, Cherry Hills Village officials directed city staff to expedite a traffic signal warrant study that had already been scheduled for discussion at a March 17 council meeting. The study will constitute a formal engineering analysis measuring traffic volume, crash history, pedestrian activity, and vehicle delays, with the Colorado Department of Transportation ultimately making the final call on whether a signal is installed.

Greenwood Village spokesperson Megan Copenhaver said the two cities have been evaluating the intersection and will continue working together. CDOT did not respond to requests for comment.

stories.headline: Nathan MacKinnon reaches 100 points and 100 even-strength goals in Colorado's 5-1 win over Anaheim
stories.summary:
Colorado Avalanche center Nathan MacKinnon hit two century marks in Tuesday night's dominant 5-1 victory in Anaheim, recording his 100th point of the season while also registering his 100th even-strength goal contribution, both achieved in his 59th game — the fastest to 100 points in franchise history and his fourth consecutive 100-point season.

MacKinnon's even-strength numbers are historically dominant: the Avalanche have outscored opponents 100-37 at even strength with him on the ice this season, a plus-63 differential that dwarfs the nearest comparable in the league. Tampa Bay's Nikita Kucherov is next at plus-34, while Edmonton is only plus-10 with Connor McDavid on the ice.

MacKinnon sits second in league scoring behind McDavid and is in contention for the Art Ross Trophy, the Maurice 'Rocket' Richard Trophy as top goal scorer, and potentially a second Hart Trophy as league MVP. Observers note that by certain advanced metrics, MacKinnon is the best player in the world at even strength by a significant margin.

The milestone 100th point came when MacKinnon set up captain Gabe Landeskog for a one-timer in the third period. Colorado's 40-win pace and MacKinnon's individual production have kept the Avalanche atop the Central Division standings.

stories.headline: Avalanche acquire defenseman Nick Blankenburg from Nashville Predators ahead of trade deadline
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche acquired defenseman Nick Blankenburg from the Nashville Predators in exchange for a fifth-round pick in the 2027 NHL Draft, the team announced officially. The move adds depth on the blue line as the Avalanche make a push toward the postseason.

Blankenburg, who had been with Nashville this season, gives Colorado additional options on defense as the team manages its roster heading into the final stretch of the regular season. The Avalanche have been among the league's best teams this year, leading the Central Division.

The trade is a low-cost addition, with Colorado giving up only a late-round pick to shore up their defensive group. It follows a pattern of calculated moves by the organization to supplement their roster without sacrificing high-value assets.

stories.headline: Nuggets sign Colorado State alum David Roddy to two-way contract, waive Tamar Bates
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets signed former Colorado State forward David Roddy to a two-way contract Wednesday to add wing depth for the final stretch of the regular season, a league source told The Denver Post. To create the roster spot, the team waived rookie guard Tamar Bates, who has been sidelined since December with a fractured foot.

Roddy, 24, was drafted 23rd overall in 2022 and has appeared in 168 NBA games across stints with the Grizzlies, Suns, Hawks, 76ers, and Rockets. Most recently he was averaging 14.4 points and 7.2 rebounds in 20 games with the Raptors 905, Toronto's G League affiliate.

The Nuggets moved to add size on the wing after their other two-way slots and the 15th roster spot were all occupied by guards following the signing of point guard Tyus Jones earlier in the week. Roddy played three seasons at Colorado State, earning Mountain West Player of the Year honors and helping lead the Rams to a No. 6 NCAA Tournament seed.

Denver (38-24) is currently fifth in the Western Conference standings and looking to stabilize its playoff positioning with a depleted roster due to injuries.

stories.headline: Aaron Gordon targeting return to Nuggets lineup as soon as Friday against Knicks
stories.summary:
Nuggets forward Aaron Gordon participated in limited drills alongside Peyton Watson during a light Wednesday practice, with The Athletic reporting he is targeting a return as early as Friday's home game against the New York Knicks. Gordon has missed 40 consecutive games after re-aggravating a right hamstring strain on January 23 in Milwaukee.

Coach David Adelman said the team is proceeding with caution given the soft-tissue nature of the injury and the stakes of the remaining regular season. Gordon was able to perform a full workout with assistant coaches after the main practice session, involving more physical activity than Watson, who remains further from a return.

Denver's defensive rating is 8.2 points per 100 possessions better with Gordon on the court than off it this season. Adelman noted that Gordon's presence in the dunker spot is a key element of the team's half-court offense alongside Nikola Jokic and Jamal Murray.

After Thursday's game against the Lakers and a potential Friday return against the Knicks, the Nuggets have the weekend off before a Monday rematch at Oklahoma City. Adelman said Gordon's availability for back-to-back games will be evaluated situationally going forward.

stories.headline: Broncos emerge as frontrunners for Kenneth Walker as NFL free agency approaches
stories.summary:
The Denver Broncos are positioning themselves as a top destination for Seattle Seahawks running back Kenneth Walker, the reigning Super Bowl MVP, according to NFL on CBS insider Jonathan Jones. Walker is expected to hit free agency and command between $12 and $16 million per year, with the Broncos, Chiefs, Jaguars, and Giants among the interested teams. Jones described Denver as an 'obvious landing spot' for the running back.

The Broncos had also pursued New York Jets running back Breece Hall, but the Jets placed the franchise tag on Hall in part because they feared Denver would make an offer they could not match. With Hall off the board, reports indicate Denver has turned its attention to Walker as its primary target.

Denver also made a last-minute bid for veteran David Montgomery and has been linked to Jacksonville's Travis Etienne Jr. and free agent JK Dobbins, who spent last season with the Broncos. Walker would likely pair with second-year back RJ Harvey if signed.

Legal tampering opens Monday, meaning the pace of reported activity around Denver's backfield pursuit is expected to accelerate over the coming days.

stories.headline: Multiple NFL teams inquiring about Broncos backup quarterback Jarrett Stidham
stories.summary:
Several NFL teams have contacted the Denver Broncos about the availability of backup quarterback Jarrett Stidham, according to The Athletic's Dianna Russini. The Vikings, Packers, and Rams have been identified as clubs with potential interest in the 29-year-old signal-caller, who has served as Denver's backup for three seasons.

Stidham's most notable moment as a Bronco came when he started in the AFC Championship Game after Bo Nix was injured, and he has been credited as a key figure in Nix's development. He has not thrown a regular-season pass in two years but is regarded as one of the better backup options in the league.

Minnesota is seeking a veteran to compete with J.J. McCarthy, Green Bay needs to replace departing Malik Willis, and the Rams may look to upgrade behind Matthew Stafford following Jimmy Garoppolo's impending free agency. Any trade would likely return modest compensation given Stidham's backup role.

stories.headline: Two Denver siblings, ages 12 and 15, reported missing from Park Hill neighborhood
stories.summary:
The Colorado Bureau of Investigation issued a Missing Indigenous Person Alert Wednesday for siblings Ariauna Brown, 12, and Andrew Brown, 15, who were last seen Monday morning near East 33rd Avenue and Dahlia Street in Denver's Park Hill neighborhood. Both children are Indigenous.

Ariauna is described as 5 feet tall and 100 pounds with brown hair and brown eyes, last seen wearing a red shirt, black sweatpants, and white shoes. Andrew is described as 5 feet 6 inches tall and 126 pounds with black hair and brown eyes, last seen in a white shirt, black and red jacket, and black sweatpants with a spider graphic.

Anyone with information on the children's whereabouts is asked to call 911 or the Denver Police Department at 720-913-2000.

stories.headline: Ruby Hill Yard closes early for the season as Denver's warm, dry winter depletes snowpack
stories.summary:
Ruby Hill Yard, the free ski and snowboarding venue operated as a partnership between Denver Parks & Recreation and Winter Park Resort, has closed for the 2025-26 season ahead of schedule due to unseasonably warm and dry winter conditions across the Denver metro area. The facility typically remains open through the end of March when weather permits.

Denver's February averaged 42.1 degrees — the third-warmest February on record since 1872 — and recorded only a trace of snowfall, tying the least-snowy February in city history, last matched in 2009. Colorado and surrounding states have reported their lowest statewide snowpack since record-keeping began in the early 1980s.

City officials noted that despite the difficult season, Denver Parks & Recreation and Winter Park were still able to offer free snowboarding lessons and host Olympic athletes at the park. Officials said they look forward to welcoming the community back next season.

The closure is connected to broader climate concerns for Colorado, with water managers and outdoor recreation operators monitoring the historically low snowpack as the spring runoff season approaches.

stories.headline: Denver to see 1-3 inches of snow Friday; mountains face winter storm warnings
stories.summary:
A storm system is set to bring a rain-snow mix to the Denver metro area and eastern plains on Friday, with the 9NEWS Weather Impact Team issuing a Weather Impact Alert for the day. Snow accumulation in Denver is forecast at 1-3 inches, with heavier totals possible west and south of downtown. The strongest precipitation window for the metro is expected Friday morning.

In the mountains, Winter Storm Warnings and Winter Weather Advisories are posted from midnight Thursday through Friday night, with 6-14 inches of snow possible across Colorado's northern mountains including Berthoud Pass, Rocky Mountain National Park, Summit County, and Indian Peaks. Travel in the mountains is expected to be treacherous at times Thursday night into Friday.

Before the storm arrives, elevated fire danger is forecast for Thursday across the Denver area and eastern plains, with fire weather warnings posted south and east of downtown due to low relative humidity and gusty winds. Denver temperatures are expected to drop from the high 60s Thursday to the low 30s by Friday morning.

The storm follows one of Denver's warmest, driest Februarys on record and arrives amid ongoing concerns about historically low statewide snowpack.

stories.headline: Tower Plaza near Denver International Airport to add grocery store and boutique hotel
stories.summary:
A mixed-use development called Tower Plaza near Peña Boulevard is expanding its program to include grocery and boutique hotel uses, according to the Denver Business Journal. The 4-acre site was acquired for $3.4 million in 2024.

The addition of a hotel component to what was already a retail-anchored project signals growing confidence in the commercial corridor near Denver International Airport, which has seen increasing development interest in recent years. Specific details on the hotel brand, grocery tenant, and construction timeline were not disclosed.

The project adds to a pipeline of development activity near DIA as the airport and its surrounding area continue to attract investment following recent expansions including the newly opened Delta Sky Club lounge.

stories.headline: Denver Restaurant Week kicks off Friday with 270-plus participating metro eateries
stories.summary:
Denver Restaurant Week begins Friday and runs through March 15, with more than 270 restaurants across the metro area offering special multi-course menus at fixed price points of $25, $35, $45, or $55. The event is presented by OpenTable and organized by the Colorado Restaurant Association.

Participating establishments range from Michelin-starred kitchens to James Beard Award-winning chefs, making it one of the region's most prominent annual dining promotions. Diners can browse participating restaurants and make reservations at denverrestaurantweek.com.

The event coincides with a busy late-winter weekend in the region that also includes the Monte Vista Crane Festival in the San Luis Valley, the return of Leadville Skijoring along Harrison Avenue, and the opening of a major Ana María Hernando exhibition at the Museum of Contemporary Art Denver.

weather.notable: About 13 degrees above the average high for this time of year!

joke.joke: I think I did pretty well, considering I started out with nothing but a bunch of blank paper.

topStory.headline: Spanish PM Sánchez Defies Trump Trade Threat Over Iran War, Refuses Base Access
topStory.summary:
Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez publicly rebuked U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday, vowing that Spain would not become complicit in the expanding military conflict in the Middle East despite explicit American threats to sever all trade relations with the country. Sánchez distilled his government's stance in three words — 'No to war' — and said Spain would not yield 'simply out of fear of reprisals from someone,' in an unmistakable reference to Trump's warnings.

The confrontation escalated after Trump, speaking Tuesday during a meeting with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, declared that Spain had been 'terrible' for refusing the United States permission to use jointly operated military bases in southern Spain to support ongoing U.S. attacks against Iran. Trump said he had directed Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to 'cut off all dealings' with Spain, a remarkable threat directed at a NATO ally and European Union member state.

In his Wednesday address, Sánchez called simultaneously on the United States, Israel, and Iran to halt the conflict before it spiraled further, warning that the situation amounted to playing 'Russian roulette with the destiny of millions.' He cautioned that no one could predict the full consequences of the current fighting, and said the world should be prepared for the possibility of a prolonged war with significant casualties and serious global economic fallout.

Sánchez pointedly invoked the 2003 U.S.-led invasion of Iraq — which his conservative predecessor José María Aznar supported — as a cautionary parallel, arguing that military campaigns launched with stated objectives of security and democracy had instead generated widespread instability. He also appeared to take indirect aim at leaders who, in his characterization, use the 'smokescreen of war' to distract from domestic failures while benefiting a select few who profit from military spending over social investment.

Later Wednesday, the Spanish government categorically denied any shift in its position, signaling that Madrid intends to hold its ground despite the American pressure. The standoff represents one of the sharpest public rifts between Washington and a Western European ally since the current conflict with Iran began, and raises questions about the durability of U.S. relationships with partners unwilling to support its military operations in the region.
